seo_title: How to Achieve Better Expressivity in State-Space Models with Flash…
meta_description: Discover how Flash PD-SSM outperforms traditional state-space models in terms of expressivity and efficiency, enabling better results in machine learning tasks.
body_html:
Direct answer: State-space models face a fundamental trade-off between efficiency and expressivity, with unstructured transition matrices enabling maximal expressivity but at a high compute and memory cost.

Key Benefits of Flash PD-SSMComparable throughput to widely-used structured SSMs with significantly better expressivity guaranteesIncreased efficiency in runtime and memory consumption compared to traditional SSMsFaster training times and improved model accuracy on various tasksExpressivity and Efficiency Trade-offStructured Transition Matrix FormEfficiencyExpressivityUnstructured transition matricesNoYesMost structured transition matrix formsYesNoAdvantages of Flash PD-SSMFlash PD-SSM maintains a trainable set of structured sparse matrices, enabling FSA expressiveness at the level of unstructured matrices while maintaining efficiency required for training models at scale.

source.title: Flash PD-SSM: Memory-Optimized Structured Sparse State-Space Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new type of state-space model called Flash PD-SSM that balances efficiency with expressivity. Unlike traditional structured sparse state-space models, which are highly efficient but limited in their ability to capture complex patterns, Flash PD-SSM uses a trainable set of matrices to achieve comparable performance while maintaining better expressivity guarantees. The model has been validated on various tasks and shown to outperform other SSM methods, particularly in multivariate time-series tasks. It also demonstrates improved throughput and reduced memory consumption compared to existing models.
